india_trends.title: $40 Billion from Google. $25 Billion from Amazon. Anthropic is now the most fought-over AI company on Earth
india_trends.summary: Google has committed up to $40 billion to Anthropic, days after Amazon pledged $25 billion, confirming the Claude maker as the AI industry's most coveted startup.

india_trends.title: Vedanta Demerger: How to trade the Anil Agarwal-led stock ahead of record date?
india_trends.summary: Ahead of the Vedanta demerger record date, Vedanta's shares are expected to be volatile. Long-term investors may accumulate shares if valuations are appealing, while short-term traders should focus on the pre-record date rallyand ensure tight stop losses.&nbsp;

india_trends.title: AI Chip Surge Elevates Taiwan, Korea in Global Equity Rankings
india_trends.summary: The artificial intelligence boom has triggered a seismic reshuffling of global equity markets, with Taiwan and South Korea muscling past European nations one by one.

india_trends.title: APCO in talks to sell Z-Morh tunnel in J&K to Alpha Alternatives for $267 mn
india_trends.summary: The 6.5-km- tunnel, which ensures uninterrupted military logistics and year-round civilian access to Ladakh by bypassing an avalanche-prone stretch, was built at ₹2,400 crore and inaugurated in 2025.

india_trends.title: Oil Declines on Renewed Optimism for US-Iran Negotiations
india_trends.summary: Oil prices fell on fresh hopes that elusive peace talks between the US and Iran may materialize after all and pave the way for the resumption of energy flows though the vital Strait of Hormuz.

india_trends.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq close at records on tech lift, Iran peace talk hopes
india_trends.summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reached record highs Friday, fueled by optimism for U.S.-Iran peace talks and a surge in Intel shares. Semiconductor stocks continued their rally, with Intel hitting a record. Attention now shifts to the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting for clues on interest rate cuts.

india_trends.title: ITC to conclude Rs 3,498-crore Century Pulp buy next week
india_trends.summary: The transaction will be concluded next week after certain leases are transferred from Aditya Birla Real Estate, which houses Century Pulp and Paper, to ITC, the people said. ITC and Aditya Birla Group did not respond to ET's queries till press time.

india_trends.title: Hindustan Zinc plans to double output by 2030, may revive demerger plan
india_trends.summary: In 2023, the board of Hindustan Zinc had proposed splitting its business into different verticals, but the plan was opposed by the Government of India on concerns that it may not be beneficial for minority shareholders. india_trends.summary: Indian car buyers are shifting towards electric vehicles. Concerns over fuel availability and price hikes due to the West Asia conflict are driving this trend. india_trends.title: Forex reserves rise to $703 billion; rupee slips 14 paise
india_trends.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a significant jump of $2.3 billion, reaching $703 billion. india_trends.title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman flags AI cyber threat, asks banks to act
india_trends.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has directed the Indian Banks' Association to help banks combat emerging cybersecurity threats from Anthropic's Claude Mythos AI. india_trends.title: Govt plans new TTDF with higher outlay by October to boost telecom R&D, says Pemmasani
india_trends.summary: The government is set to launch an enhanced Telecom Technology Development Fund (TTDF) with a larger outlay by October, according to Minister of State for Telecom Pemmasani Chandra Sekhar. The ministry has already allocated approximately ₹540 crore to over 100 companies under the existing TTDF schem

india_trends.title: Boost R&D or lag in 6G race, MoS Chandra Sekhar Pemmasani tells telcos as revenues surge
india_trends.summary: Minister Chandra Sekhar Pemmasani urged Indian telecom operators to boost R&D spending to shape next-generation mobile networks, noting their low investment compared to global giants. The government is preparing for 6G with initiatives like the Bharat 6G Vision and regulatory sandboxes to foster

india_trends.title: Credit flows jump 38% in FY26 as RBI easing boosts demand
india_trends.summary: India's formal loan systems saw a significant surge in FY26, marking a strong revival in borrowing demand. This growth followed a contraction in the previous year. The Reserve Bank of India's policy rate cuts and liquidity infusion spurred lending. Credit expansion reached ₹44.6 lakh crore. Outstand
